# UML-MCP — LLM-oriented overview

Teaser
Improve your diagramming skills with PlantUML, Mermaid, D2 (and Kroki) by creating optimized diagrams for various purposes using Sequence, Use Case, Class, Activity, Component, State, Object, Deployment, Mermaid, D2, Graphviz, ERD, BPMN, C4.

Prompt hint
[DIAGRAM TYPE] — Sequence, Use Case, Class, Activity, Component, State, Object, Deployment, Mermaid, D2, Graphviz, ERD, BPMN, C4.
[ELEMENT TYPE] — Actors, Messages, Objects, Classes, Interfaces, Components, States, Nodes, Edges, Links, Frames, Constraints, Entities, Relationships, Tasks, Events, Modules.
[PURPOSE] — Communication, Planning, Design, Analysis, Modeling, Documentation, Implementation, Testing, Debugging.
[DIAGRAMMING TOOL] — PlantUML, Mermaid, D2 (Kroki for rendering).

Recommended: use sequential thinking for complex diagrams. Use the uml_diagram_with_thinking prompt to get step-by-step planning (decide diagram type, purpose, and elements), then generate code and call generate_uml.

---

UML-MCP is a diagram generation server that implements the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It lets AI assistants and other MCP clients create diagrams (UML, Mermaid, D2, Graphviz, ERD, etc.) via tools and resources.

## What it does

- Generates diagrams from code/descriptions using PlantUML, Kroki, Mermaid, D2, Graphviz backends.
- Exposes MCP tools (e.g. generate_uml, generate_class_diagram, generate_sequence_diagram) and resources (uml://types, uml://templates, uml://examples, uml://formats, uml://server-info, uml://workflow).
- Output formats: SVG, PNG, PDF (and others depending on diagram type).

## Diagram types

- UML: class, sequence, activity, use case, state, component, deployment, object.
- Other: Mermaid, D2, Graphviz, ERD, BlockDiag, BPMN, C4 (PlantUML).

## MCP tools (summary)

- generate_uml — diagram_type, code, output_dir.
- Type-specific: generate_class_diagram, generate_sequence_diagram, generate_activity_diagram, generate_usecase_diagram, etc.
- generate_mermaid_diagram, generate_d2_diagram, generate_graphviz_diagram, generate_erd_diagram.
- sequentialthinking — plan/verify diagram design step-by-step before generating.

Full tool list and parameters: docs/api/tools.md

## MCP resources

- uml://types — diagram types.
- uml://templates — diagram templates.
- uml://examples — examples.
- uml://formats — output formats.
- uml://server-info — server info.
- uml://workflow — recommended workflow for complex diagrams (use uml_diagram_with_thinking or sequentialthinking before generate_uml).

## Configuration

- MCP_OUTPUT_DIR / UML_MCP_OUTPUT_DIR — where to save diagrams (default: ./output).
- KROKI_SERVER, PLANTUML_SERVER — backend URLs.
- USE_LOCAL_KROKI, USE_LOCAL_PLANTUML — use local backends.
- DISABLE_THOUGHT_LOGGING — set true to reduce sequentialthinking log noise.

Details: docs/configuration.md

## Running

- MCP server (stdio): python server.py (from project root).
- MCP over HTTP: python server.py --transport http --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000.
- List tools: python server.py --list-tools.

## Project layout

- server.py — MCP server entry point.
- mcp_core/ — server, config, tools, resources, prompts.
- plantuml/, kroki/, mermaid/, D2/ — diagram backends.
- config/ — example MCP client configs (Cursor, Claude Desktop).
- docs/ — documentation (this file, api/tools.md, configuration.md, installation.md, integrations/, testing.md).

## Deploy and publish

- Deploy to Vercel and publish on Smithery: docs/integrations/vercel_smithery.md
- Smithery docs index: https://smithery.ai/docs/llms.txt

## More docs

- Installation: docs/installation.md
- API / tools: docs/api/tools.md
- Configuration: docs/configuration.md
- Cursor: docs/integrations/cursor.md
- Claude Desktop: docs/integrations/claude_desktop.md
- Testing: docs/testing.md
